title: PagerDuty postmortem summary
description: One record per postmortem / retrospective over a bounded look-back window: the postmortem's opaque id, the linked incident id, the review status, the created / published timestamps, and a corrective-action ROLLUP (the count of tracked action items plus the completed / open split). Pulled read-only from the PagerDuty REST API (postmortems endpoint, since/until window). META-FACTS ONLY — proves a review EXISTS for an incident and that corrective actions are tracked, NEVER the postmortem narrative body, timeline free-text, root-cause prose, an action item's operator-authored title or description, customer data, or any responder personal contact detail. The over-collection boundary is structural: the connector's record types have no field that can hold the narrative, and a reflection guard fails the build if one is added. Proves incidents are reviewed and corrective actions tracked, feeding the IR continuous-improvement loop (SOC 2 CC7.5 'recover from identified security incidents'; SCF IRO-13 Root-Cause Analysis, IRO-09 Incident Reporting). The connector reports a descriptive review posture; the platform evaluator owns the final pass/fail per (control, scope). x-default-scf-anchors are default mapping hints flagged for maintainer recheck (OQ #9).

postmortem_id.description: PagerDuty-native stable identifier of the postmortem / retrospective (an opaque ID, not a credential).

incident_id.description: PagerDuty-native stable identifier of the incident this review is about (an opaque ID). Ties the review to the incident; not a credential.

status.description: Postmortem review lifecycle status. A normalized enum, never a free-text status string.

published_at.description: When the postmortem was published (RFC 3339); absent if not yet published.

action_item_count.description: Number of corrective-action items tracked on the postmortem. A COUNT only — never an item title or description.

action_items_completed.description: Number of tracked corrective-action items in a completed state.

action_items_open.description: Number of tracked corrective-action items not yet completed (action_item_count minus action_items_completed).
